meta.voice: AZ Law Now — data-first, contractions, no em-dashes, peer-to-peer.
meta.rules: Caption: lead with the strongest hook in first 125 chars (truncate point). Body up to 2200 chars but tight is better. NO clickable links in caption (IG strips them) — say 'link in profile' or 'full investigation in our profile bio link'. Hashtags go in the FIRST COMMENT, not the caption (cleaner look + same algorithmic effect).

posts.caption:
Buckeye's own engineering report said a roundabout would cut injury crashes 41.7% at Durango and Yuma. The City picked the cheaper signalized design. 15.4% reduction.

A 26.3-point safety gap. $1.4M saved up front. Built into the next decade of crash reports.

Full investigation with the CMF table, FHWA benchmarks, and Buckeye's 33% population growth math: link in our profile bio.

60% of nursing home sexual abuse victims have dementia.
80% of perpetrators are caregivers.
Only 30% of incidents reach law enforcement.

A Mesa caregiver was arrested April 21 for alleged abuse of a wheelchair-bound dementia patient. The family caught it on video.

The case fits a national pattern. HB2228 is the AZ bill that closes the registry gap — currently in the Senate.

Full investigation with primary sources, statutory references, and what families can do: link in our profile bio.

FHWA data on signalized intersections converted to roundabouts: 46% reduction in fatal and serious-injury crashes.

Buckeye had this data in front of them at Durango and Yuma. Picked the cheaper option anyway.

The math doesn't care about public unfamiliarity. Read the investigation: link in our profile bio.

Buckeye, Arizona. 2020 population: 93,741. 2024 estimate: 114,334.

32.95% growth in four years. Top tier of fastest-growing US cities.

More people. More AADT. Same arterial intersections. The road geometry decisions made today carry for decades.

Why the City's choice at Durango and Yuma compounds: link in our profile bio.

If your loved one is in an Arizona long-term care facility, three things matter immediately:

1. Pull the AZDHS inspection record. It's public.
2. Document anything that doesn't sit right. Photos, dates, behavior changes.
3. APS hotline: 1-877-767-2385.

The data on what happens when families don't catch it early is the reason HB2228 exists.

Full guide with statutory references and reporting paths: link in our profile bio.

Arizona's largest university-affiliated charter network is also a separate Arizona nonprofit corporation. Its FY24 IRS 990 reports a $5.46M operating loss on $82.4M revenue.

The FY21 audit, filed at ade.az.gov, discloses a related-party operating lease on a school-site building. $11.5M right-of-use asset. Agreement runs through September 2032.

Note 9 names the counterparty. That's the next pull.

Full investigation with the audit text, federal-funds schedule, and the public-records path under A.R.S. 39-121: link in our profile bio.

The federal Walgreens opioid settlement was announced in April 2025. Up to $350 million. Covered conduct from August 2012 through March 2023.

Arizona lost 1,659 people in the most recent 12-month surveillance window. 2,846 in 2021.

Which Arizona Walgreens stores filled the prescriptions covered by the settlement? The list is in the Eastern District of New York federal docket.

Three records close the Arizona accountability gap: the consent decree exhibits, the DEA Phoenix Federal Register decisions, and the Arizona State Board of Pharmacy meeting minutes. All three are public.

Full investigation: link in our profile bio.

Buckeye's own engineering report projected a roundabout would cut serious-injury crashes 41.7% at Durango and Yuma.

The City picked the signalized design: 15.4% reduction. A 26.3 percentage-point safety gap, traded for $1.4 million in concrete.

Full investigation — link in bio.

#Arizona #PublicSafety #InfrastructureMatters #Buckeye #MaricopaCounty #DataJournalism #AZLawNow
posts.first_comment: Methodology: City of Buckeye Traffic Engineering Report ENGCIP-25-0004 (2025). Crash Modification Factor analysis. FHWA Proven Safety Countermeasures cross-reference. Reporting by Brendan Franks. Read the full investigation at azlawnow.com/investigations/buckeye-durango-yuma-roundabout-rejected/

posts.caption:
Arizona's Board for Private Postsecondary Education licensed 237 private career schools in fiscal year 2025. 37 got adverse actions. 9 schools closed.

Full investigation, link in bio.

#Arizona #StudentLoans #ConsumerProtection #HigherEducation #Title4 #DataJournalism #AZLawNow
posts.first_comment: Methodology: Arizona State Board for Private Postsecondary Education FY2025 Annual Report cross-referenced against the 2023 Auditor General performance audit (Report 94-2). Reporting by Brendan Franks. Read the full investigation at azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-career-schools-37-adverse-actions/

posts.caption:
51 daycare facilities cited 3 or more times across 4 West Valley cities, 2023 to 2025.

Staff-ratio violations are 40% of all West Valley citations. Repeat violators can operate for years.

Full investigation, link in bio.

#Arizona #ChildSafety #ParentingMatters #DaycareSafety #WestValley #DataJournalism #AZLawNow
posts.first_comment: Methodology: AZ DHS CareCheck inspection records 2023-2025, supplemented with public records requests for unposted reports. Per-facility violation history compiled from Arizona Administrative Code Title 9, Chapter 5 deficiency citations. Reporting by Brendan Franks. Read the full investigation at azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-daycare-violations/
